title: capture: drop replication slot on graceful shutdown
description:
## Problem

When `tap capture` shuts down gracefully (SIGINT/SIGTERM), the replication slot (`tap_slot` by default) is NOT dropped. `PgConnection::close()` at `capture.rs:432` only drops the client connection — the slot persists as `active=false` in PostgreSQL forever.

## Impact

Stale slots retain WAL from their `restart_lsn` onward, unbounded. On small disks this causes 100% disk usage (see Aiven RCA — 1056 MB WAL from ~18 MB data).

## Acceptance

- [ ] Add a `--persist-slot` flag (default: false) to `tap capture`
- [ ] When `--persist-slot=false` (default), drop the replication slot during graceful shutdown
- [ ] When `--persist-slot=true`, keep the slot for resume capability
- [ ] Update existing tests to cover both paths

## Files

- `crates/tap-cli/src/commands/capture.rs` (lines 427-434)
- `crates/tap-core/src/postgres/connection.rs` (add `drop_replication_slot()` method)
- `crates/tap-core/src/config.rs` (add `persist_slot` field)
close_reason: Superseded: slot retention is intentional for resume. The v0.1.0 spec proposed a config flag but design decision for v0.2.0 is to keep the slot and monitor instead. Monitoring covered by tap-uxb. Not a bug — missing feature from v0.1.0 spec that doesn't align with v0.2.0 direction.

title: fix(migration): wrap apply_migration+set_version in single transaction
description:
## Problem

In migration.rs, apply_migration(conn, version) runs DDL via execute_batch and set_version(conn, version) inserts into schema_version as two separate uncommitted calls. SQLite execute_batch does not implicitly wrap multiple statements in one transaction.

If the process is killed (OOM, SIGKILL, power loss) after execute_batch(MIGRATION_V2) commits but before set_version writes version=2, the DB is structurally at v2 but current_version() still returns 1. Next StateStore::open() re-runs MIGRATION_V2, hits ALTER TABLE offsets RENAME COLUMN committed_lsn TO position on a column that no longer exists — permanent crash loop, no self-healing path.

## Fix

Move set_version inside a transaction that also covers the DDL in apply_migration. The outer migrate() loop should no longer call set_version separately.

## Acceptance Criteria

- apply_migration wraps DDL + version bump in a single BEGIN/COMMIT
- If execute_batch fails, ROLLBACK is called
- A test verifies that a simulated crash between DDL and set_version does not leave the DB in an inconsistent state — re-running migrate() succeeds cleanly

## References

Found in PR #17 code review. File: crates/tap-core/src/state/migration.rs:80
close_reason: fix: wrap DDL + version bump in single transaction

title: Fix: wal2json checkpoint never written — empty LSN silently skips write_offset
description:
## Problem

In capture.rs L354-355, checkpoint_lsn is extracted via e.source.lsn.0.parse::<postgres::Lsn>().ok(). The wal2json decoder sets source.lsn = EventLsn(String::new()) because wal2json has no per-event LSNs. postgres::Lsn::from_str("") returns Err (confirmed by tests). So .ok() → None, the guard at L367 if let (Some(lsn), Some(tx_id)) never passes, and write_offset is never called for wal2json sessions.

Result: on restart, replication replays from the beginning — all events re-delivered unboundedly.

## Fix

Branch on plugin type OR have decoder.decode() return a separate commit_lsn output so the checkpoint path gets the LSN regardless of event format. At minimum, log a warning when checkpoint is silently skipped due to missing LSN (currently there is no indication).

## Acceptance Criteria
- write_offset is called after each committed wal2json transaction
- Restart after wal2json session resumes from last committed transaction, not from scratch
- Test: integration test that runs wal2json decode → captures N events → restarts → verifies no duplicate delivery
close_reason: wal2json checkpoint fix: DecodeResult + ReplicationStream::current_lsn() LSN fallback chain; is_final checkpoint: last_committed_lsn tracking + final checkpoint on shutdown; decode error handling: flush() + byte count + record_skipped_lsn. All 200 unit tests passing.

title: [PR #13] C5: Fix SASLResponse wire format (extra Int32 prefix breaks SCRAM auth)
acceptance_criteria: Full SCRAM handshake against a real PG (PG 14+, scram-sha-256 required) succeeds. Test: hand-rolled mock server that verifies the client-final bytes match the spec, plus an integration test with testcontainers.
close_reason: Fixed: SASLResponse wire format corrected, build_sasl_response() helper extracted, regression test added, 155/155 tests pass

title: [PR #13] C4: Reject null bytes in startup-message user/dbname parameters
description:
**File:** crates/tap-core/src/replication.rs L222-230

**Bug:** `let user_param = format!("user\0{}\0", config.user);`. Rust strings allow embedded \0. A `user` value of `"alice\0options=-c\log_statement=all"` produces a startup message that the server parses as two parameters: `user=alice` and `options=-c log_statement=all`. Parameter-injection / wire-confusion bug. Same for `dbname`.

**Fix:** Validate `config.user` and `config.dbname` at `SourceConfig::validate`, rejecting \0 and any byte outside printable ASCII. Or escape \0 to `''` before writing.

**Discovered in:** PR #13 review (id 4432763377), commit 42b43bc
acceptance_criteria: Config validation rejects user/dbname containing \0 or non-printable ASCII bytes with a clear error.
close_reason: Fixed in commit 94c7669: null-byte validation added to SourceConfig::validate, 2 tests added, 152/152 unit tests pass

title: [PR #13] C3: Escape/validate slot_name, publication, plugin in START_REPLICATION query
description:
**File:** crates/tap-core/src/replication.rs L196-199

**Bug:** `slot_name`, `plugin`, and `publication` are interpolated raw into the SQL string. The old stub at `connection.rs:404` called `validate_identifier(slot_name, "slot_name")?` before passing it down. The new `start()` and `start_replication` wrapper both dropped that call — only the top-level `SourceConfig::validate` runs validation, and only when config is loaded from TOML. A direct API call to `PgConnection::start_replication(s, p, l, pl)` with `'x"; DROP TABLE x; --'` will reach the wire unescaped. A `'` in `plugin` or `publication` does the same.

**Fix:** Add `crate::config::validate_identifier(slot_name, "slot_name")?`, ditto for `publication` and `plugin`, inside `replication::start` before constructing the query.

**Discovered in:** PR #13 review (id 4432763377), commit 42b43bc
acceptance_criteria: Property test: 100 random slot_name/publication strings (including SQL injection attempts like `' ; DROP TABLE x; --`) either pass validation or return Err before reaching the wire.
close_reason: Fixed: validate_identifier calls added to replication::start(), START_REPLICATION option names fixed for pgoutput (publication_names), clippy clean, 154/154 tests pass

title: [PR #13] C2: TLS verifier must honor SslMode (no silent VerifyFull downgrade)
description:
**File:** crates/tap-core/src/replication.rs L175

**Bug:** `native_tls::TlsConnector::builder().build()` uses the least strict config: no root store loaded, no hostname check, no cert verification. The same code path runs for `SslMode::Require`, `VerifyCa`, and `VerifyFull`. A user who set `ssl_mode = "verify-full"` gets an unverified TLS connection — vulnerable to active MITM, defeating the security purpose.

**Fix:** Branch on `ssl_mode`:
- Disable → no TLS
- Require → TLS without verification
- VerifyCa → load system CAs
- VerifyFull → VerifyCa + hostname check (note: `native-tls` 0.2 doesn't expose hostname verification directly — consider switching to `rustls`/`tokio-rustls`, or document the gap loudly)

**Discovered in:** PR #13 review (id 4432763377), commit 42b43bc
acceptance_criteria: Connecting with ssl_mode=verify-full against a self-signed server is REJECTED, not silently accepted. Test: spin PG with self-signed cert, connect with verify-full, assert Err.
close_reason: Fixed: SslMode now honored — Require skips verification, VerifyCa checks CA only, VerifyFull checks all; 155/155 tests pass

title: [PR #13] C1: Add Postgres SSLRequest handshake before TLS
description:
**File:** crates/tap-core/src/replication.rs L171-183

**Bug:** Code goes straight from `TcpStream::connect` to TLS handshake. The Postgres postmaster does NOT auto-initiate TLS — it requires the client to first send an 8-byte `SSLRequest` (length=8, code=80877103), then read a single byte 'S'/'N' to know whether to expect TLS bytes. Without that pre-exchange, the postmaster sees ClientHello bytes, fails to parse them, and closes the connection. This breaks TLS against any default-configured Postgres.

**Fix:** Implement the SSLRequest dance. Send 8 bytes, read 1 byte, branch on 'S'/'N', then call `connector.connect` only on the 'S' branch. Reference: `postgres-native-tls`'s `MakeTlsConnector` does this.

**Discovered in:** PR #13 review (id 4432763377), commit 42b43bc
acceptance_criteria: Connection to PG with ssl=on succeeds over TLS. Test: start a local PG with ssl enabled, connect, assert TLS handshake completes.
close_reason: Fixed: SSLRequest added before TLS handshake, 'S'/'N' response handling, tcp made mutable, 155/155 tests pass

title: fix(release.yml): missing cross-compile linker install for Linux targets
description:
aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu requires gcc-aarch64-linux-gnu and x86_64-unknown-linux-musl requires musl-tools. Neither is pre-installed on ubuntu-24.04 runners. Cargo calls the system C linker at link time — without it, both targets fail with 'linker not found'. Two of five release targets never build.

Fix: add step before Build CLI binary:
  - name: Install cross-compilation toolchain
    if: runner.os == 'Linux'
    run: |
      sudo apt-get update -q
      sudo apt-get install -y gcc-aarch64-linux-gnu musl-tools

Reported in PR #11 review.
close_reason: All 5 release pipeline bugs fixed. Details: tap-0az (pushd/popd SDK_DIR in release.sh), tap-a63 (unset RUSTFLAGS), tap-dca (cross-compile linker install in release.yml), tap-d83 (frozen-lockfile), tap-6g1 (SHA-pinned actions).

title: fix(release.sh): napi build runs in REPO_ROOT instead of SDK_DIR
description:
pnpm install, napi build --platform, and napi artifacts all execute from REPO_ROOT because SDK_DIR is set on L127 but never cd-into. napi reads root package.json (doesn't exist), writes .node artifacts to wrong location. Step 3 of release.sh always fails on a clean run.

Fix: pushd/popd around the SDK build block:
  pushd "$SDK_DIR"
  pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
  npx @napi-rs/cli build --platform --release
  npx @napi-rs/cli artifacts
  popd

Reported in PR #11 review.
close_reason: All 5 release pipeline bugs fixed. Details: tap-0az (pushd/popd SDK_DIR in release.sh), tap-a63 (unset RUSTFLAGS), tap-dca (cross-compile linker install in release.yml), tap-d83 (frozen-lockfile), tap-6g1 (SHA-pinned actions).

title: fix: E2E test always exits 0 even with zero CDC events
description: E2E smoke test in scripts/e2e-test.sh:197-204 prints WARNING but exits 0 when zero events captured. Need hard assertion on CDC output. Fix: change WARNING path to exit 1, add minimum event count assertion.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #10 review follow-up: E2E exit code, SlotGuard, WAL isolation, SDK config test, connection_string merge, test_name collision, CI caching, parameterized SQL, secrets

title: P9-FIX-2: JsTapConfig.connection field is dead code (CRITICAL)
description:
JsTapConfig.connection field is documented as 'Postgres connection string (overrides host/port/database/user/password)' but build_tap_config() never reads it. The function constructs the connection from individual host/port/database/user/password fields and silently ignores the connection string. Users who pass only a connection URI (the primary documented use case in the TS constructor example) get default localhost:5432 values.

**Fix:** Parse the connection URI in build_tap_config() via tokio-postgres's config parser to extract host/port/dbname/user/password, falling back to individual fields only when connection is empty.

**Location:** packages/sdk-ts/src/lib.rs:133 (JsTapConfig.connection), packages/sdk-ts/src/lib.rs:505 (build_tap_config)
close_reason: Closed

title: P9-FIX-1: normalizeConfig camelCase vs snake_case mismatch (CRITICAL)
description:
normalizeConfig() in packages/sdk-ts/src/index.ts:241 returns camelCase keys (slotName, statePath, maxBatchSize, flushIntervalMs) but napi-rs #[napi(object)] structs use Rust field names verbatim (snake_case: slot_name, state_path, max_batch_size, flush_interval_ms). All multi-word config fields silently resolve to None/0 — the default instead of the user's value. Affects JsTapConfig AND nested JsSinkConfig (maxBufferSize, heartbeatIntervalMs).

**Fix:** Either (a) emit snake_case keys in normalizeConfig, or (b) add #[napi(object, name_transform = "camelCase")] to JsTapConfig and JsSinkConfig in lib.rs.

**Location:** packages/sdk-ts/src/index.ts:241, packages/sdk-ts/src/lib.rs:67 (JsTapConfig), packages/sdk-ts/src/lib.rs:111 (JsSinkConfig)
close_reason: Closed

title: fix(cli): add SIGTERM handler for graceful shutdown in containers
close_reason: Fixed: SIGTERM handler registered via tokio::signal::unix alongside SIGINT in capture.rs:249-264

title: fix(cli): post-snapshot LSN = 0 will cause full WAL replay
description:
**Location:** `crates/tap-cli/src/commands/capture.rs:210`

**Bug:** After a successful snapshot, `start_replication()` is called with `Lsn::ZERO` instead of the snapshot's recorded LSN.

**Root cause:** `start_lsn` is a local variable set at L79-97 before the snapshot block. The snapshot returns `snapshot_result.lsn` at L165 but `start_lsn` is never reassigned. At L210, `start_lsn.unwrap_or(Lsn::ZERO)` resolves to 0.

**Impact:** When the real replication implementation lands, this replays ALL WAL changes since the beginning of time — duplicate events, memory exhaustion, potential crash.

**Fix:** After successful snapshot at L165, reassign `start_lsn = Some(snapshot_result.lsn);` before L210.

**Verification:** Add `debug_assert!(start_lsn.is_some() || !config.capture.snapshot, "snapshot ran but start_lsn is still None");` at L210. Log the actual start_lsn passed to start_replication() for operator visibility.

**Reported:** PR #8 review (comment A, confidence=100, impact=90).
design: Defense-in-depth: (1) Fix at source — update start_lsn after snapshot. (2) Add assertion. (3) Log the start LSN so zero-LSN starts are detectable. (4) When replication is wired up, integration test that verifies replication starts at the post-snapshot LSN.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] start_lsn updated to snapshot_result.lsn after successful snapshot
- [ ] debug_assert added to catch future regressions
- [ ] tracing::info! logs the start_lsn value at L210
- [ ] cargo build, cargo test, cargo clippy all pass
close_reason: Fixed: start_lsn reassigned after snapshot at capture.rs:187, debug_assert added at L223, warning on ZERO LSN at L226

title: security: add authentication to /events, /health, /status SSE endpoints
description: All three SSE server endpoints (sse.rs:320-361) have zero authentication. Any network-reachable caller can subscribe to the full real-time CDC stream (every INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE with before/after row values) via a single curl. /health and /status also leak WAL LSN positions and internal replication state. In cloud deployments binding to 0.0.0.0 this is internet-exposed by default. Fix: add axum middleware requiring Bearer or X-Api-Key header on all routes. /health may return a stripped public response (status: ok/degraded/down only) without auth.
close_reason: Fixed

title: fix: shutdown race — SSE clients miss Shutdown event before disconnect
description: In shutdown() (sse.rs:490-493), broadcast.send(shutdown_event) then immediately shutdown_tx.send(true). In sse_event_stream, tokio::select! without biased; picks ready branches pseudo-randomly. The shutdown watch fires before the bridge task drains the broadcast event, so clients close without receiving event: shutdown. Fix: add biased; to the select! in sse_event_stream (checking rx.recv() before shutdown_rx.changed()), OR encode shutdown entirely through the broadcast channel (break on SseEventType::Shutdown) to eliminate the race.
close_reason: Fixed

title: fix: replace unbounded mpsc channel in sse_event_stream with bounded channel
description: sse_event_stream() uses tokio::sync::mpsc::unbounded_channel() (sse.rs:243) to bridge the broadcast receiver to the SSE stream. The broadcast channel is bounded at max_buffer_size but the per-client mpsc queue is unlimited. A slow client accumulates the full event stream in memory during snapshot bursts (millions of events). This OOMs the process and kills the CDC pipeline. Fix: replace with mpsc::channel(config.max_buffer_size). On bounded send failure, break the bridge loop and disconnect the slow client.
close_reason: Fixed

title: fix: snapshot engine loads entire table into memory — OOM on large tables
description: snapshot_table() calls client.query() which buffers all rows into Vec<Row> before returning. A 10M-row table at 500 bytes/row = 5GB in process memory before a single event is emitted. config.batch_size is only used as a checkpoint interval counter, not to limit fetch size. Fix with a server-side cursor: DECLARE snap_cursor CURSOR FOR SELECT...; loop { FETCH batch_size FROM snap_cursor }. Also: batch_size = 0 panics at mod-zero at L459 — add validation in config.
close_reason: Fixed: server-side cursor via DECLARE/FETCH replaces client.query() which buffered all rows; uses FETCH FORWARD batch_size FROM snap_cursor in a loop. Added batch_size>0 validation in SnapshotConfig::validate().

title: fix: snapshot transaction lifetime — export_snapshot commits before snapshot_table can use it
description: export_snapshot() opens BEGIN, calls pg_export_snapshot(), then COMMITS. PostgreSQL docs: snapshot is only valid until the exporting transaction ends. snapshot_table() opens a new BEGIN and calls SET TRANSACTION SNAPSHOT with a now-dead snapshot_id — always fails with ERROR: invalid snapshot identifier. Snapshot engine emits zero rows in production. Fix requires a second non-replication connection to hold the export transaction open for the duration of the entire snapshot run, only committing after all tables are scanned. Also: pg_conn uses options='--replication=database' which forbids ordinary SQL — detect_pk_columns catalog queries and SELECT * scans need a separate regular connection regardless.
close_reason: Fixed: keeper connection now holds pg_export_snapshot() transaction open for entire snapshot run; worker connection uses SET TRANSACTION SNAPSHOT per table. Added connect_plain() to connection.rs for non-replication connections.

title: fix: missing ROLLBACK on error paths in snapshot_table and export_snapshot
description: After BEGIN in snapshot_table (L369), every ? exit (detect_pk_columns L377, row_to_json L418, channel send L452) returns Err without issuing ROLLBACK. Connection is left in aborted transaction state; next query fails with ERROR: current transaction is aborted. Same issue in export_snapshot (L265-277) if LSN query or parse fails. Fix: wrap transaction body so any error triggers client.batch_execute(ROLLBACK).await.ok() before propagating. Consider a RAII transaction guard.
close_reason: Fixed: worker table transactions use tokio_postgres::Transaction which auto-rollbacks on Drop; keeper transaction in run() wraps run_inner() with ROLLBACK on any error via match statement.

title: Audit and replace ~35 .unwrap() calls in decoder
description: Decoder has ~35 .unwrap() calls — highest density in codebase. Any malformed WAL byte in replication stream panics and crashes entire CDC pipeline. Codebase has been systematically removing unwraps since P2. Fix: protocol field unwraps → .ok_or(TapError::Decode(...)). Schema cache lookups → .ok_or_else(...). Critical for wal2json labeled experimental.
close_reason: Audit complete: all 35 .unwrap() calls are in test code only. Zero production unwraps. No changes needed.

title: Non-unique event IDs within a transaction
description: All events in a multi-DML transaction get identical IDs (format!("{}:{}", lsn, tx_id) in L381's loop). Downstream consumers using ID for deduplication silently drop events 2–N. Fix: add per-event sequence counter: format!("{}:{}:{}", lsn, tx_id, idx).
close_reason: Fix applied: event IDs now include per-transaction index via .enumerate(), format changed from "{lsn}:{tx_id}" to "{lsn}:{tx_id}:{idx}"

title: Silent data loss on nested Begin — pending_events.clear()
description: decode_begin() unconditionally calls self.pending_events.clear(), silently discarding all DML from a prior uncommitted transaction. No error, no log, no recovery. Triggered by duplicate Begin before Commit (stream reconnection edge case). Fix: warn+flush pending events before clearing, or return error. Never silently drop CDC data.
close_reason: Fix applied: pending_events.clear() now guarded with tracing::warn! when discarding uncommitted events on nested Begin

title: Negative count → OOM/process abort on malformed WAL
description: Signed-to-usize cast of read_i16/read_i32 produces usize::MAX when protocol bytes are 0xFFFF (−1). Triggers Vec::with_capacity(usize::MAX) → process abort. Affects L415 (Relation column count), L468 (Relation count), L497 (tuple column count). Fix: validate ≥0 before cast, or use read_u16/read_u32.
close_reason: Fix applied: three sites (decode_relation ncols, skip_truncate nrels, parse_tuple_data ncols) now validate positive before as usize cast, returning TapError::Decode on negative values

title: tap-fmt-fix: Fix cargo fmt failure on builder.rs L180
description: CI format check fails: assert_eq! line in test_builder_all_ops (builder.rs:180) exceeds max line width. Run cargo fmt --all to fix. Simple fix, should be done before merging PR #2.
close_reason: Closed

title: fix: tokio::task::spawn in MySqlConnection Drop panics without runtime
description:
## Problem

In connection.rs:303, the Drop impl calls tokio::task::spawn to disconnect the pool. This panics with 'there is no reactor running' in any context without an active Tokio runtime: sync unit tests (without #[tokio::test]), shutdown after runtime teardown, or non-async threads.

mysql_async::Pool already handles cleanup on its own Drop, so this explicit spawn may also be redundant.

## Solution

Guard with tokio::runtime::Handle::try_current() before spawning. If no runtime is available, pool cleanup is left to mysql_async's own Drop. Alternatively, remove the Drop impl entirely if mysql_async cleanup is sufficient.
close_reason: Fixed via PR

title: fix: encode user/password in MySqlSourceConfig::connection_url()
description:
## Problem

In config.rs:374, connection_url() formats raw user/password into a mysql:// URL string without percent-encoding. Credentials containing URL metacharacters (@, :, /) corrupt the URL authority and cause silent misparse or wrong-host connection.

Example: password p@ss → mysql://user:p@ss@localhost:3306/db → parsed as user=user:p, host=ss.

## Solution

Replace string URL construction with mysql_async::OptsBuilder, which accepts each field separately and handles encoding internally. Remove connection_url() and callers update to use opts() instead.
close_reason: Fixed via PR

title: fix: recognize ALL PRIVILEGES in MySqlConnection::check_privileges()
description:
## Problem

In connection.rs:268, check_privileges() checks SHOW GRANTS output for literal strings 'REPLICATION SLAVE', 'REPLICATION CLIENT', and 'SELECT ON'. A DBA user granted ALL PRIVILEGES ON *.* has none of these literals in their SHOW GRANTS output — they are incorrectly rejected.

Also: the SELECT ON per-DB check has a case mismatch — grants_concat is uppercased but the format string still uses lowercase self.config.dbname, so the check never matches database-specific SELECT grants.

## Solution

Check for 'ALL PRIVILEGES ON *.*' first and short-circuit all individual checks. Uppercase dbname in the format string for the per-DB SELECT check.
close_reason: Fixed via PR

title: Monitor replication slot lag in sidecar /metrics endpoint
description:
## Goal (v0.2.0)

Add replication slot WAL retention monitoring to the tap-sidecar /metrics endpoint. This is the primary defense against the Aiven 100pct disk incident: detect slot lag before it fills the disk.

## What to build

1. Add PgConnection::replication_slot_lag() method querying pg_replication_slots
2. Expose as Prometheus gauge in sidecar /metrics:
   tap_replication_slot_wal_retained_bytes{slot="tap_slot"}
3. Add WARN-level log when retention exceeds threshold (default 500 MB)
4. Include slot status in /health endpoint (active, lag_bytes)

## Files

- crates/tap-core/src/postgres/connection.rs — add slot_lag query method
- crates/tap-core/src/config.rs — add wal_retention_warning_threshold config field
- crates/tap-sidecar/src/routes/metrics.rs — add gauge
- crates/tap-sidecar/src/routes/health.rs — include slot status

## Non-Goals

- No slot-dropping logic (v0.2.0 keeps slot for resume)
- No alerting system (Prometheus alert rules are configured externally)
- MySQL equivalent not needed (MySQL has no replication slot concept)

title: Add 'tap cleanup' command to drop orphaned replication slots
description:
## Problem

If `tap capture` crashes (SIGKILL, panic) or `tap init` is run without a subsequent capture, the replication slot is orphaned. There is no built-in way to clean it up.

## Solution

Add a `tap cleanup` command that:
- Lists all replication slots matching the configured pattern (e.g., `tap_*`)
- Optionally drops all inactive ones
- Shows how much WAL each slot is retaining
- Has a `--dry-run` flag for safety

## Acceptance

- [ ] `tap cleanup --list` shows all tap-owned slots and WAL retention
- [ ] `tap cleanup --drop <slot>` drops a specific slot
- [ ] `tap cleanup --drop-all-inactive` drops all inactive tap slots
- [ ] `--dry-run` flag does not modify anything
- [ ] Tests cover list, drop, dry-run, and error paths

## Files

- `crates/tap-cli/src/commands/mod.rs` (register cleanup)
- `crates/tap-cli/src/commands/cleanup.rs` (new)
- `crates/tap-core/src/postgres/connection.rs` (add list/drop slot methods)
close_reason: Superseded: cleanup command is over-engineered for the problem. If monitoring (tap-uxb) catches WAL growth, the fix is one SQL command (SELECT pg_drop_replication_slot(...)). Not worth a CLI subcommand in v0.2.0.

title: fix(migration): drop UNIQUE constraint on offsets.position in v2
description:
## Problem

The v1 DDL defined committed_lsn TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE. SQLite's ALTER TABLE RENAME COLUMN preserves constraints, so after the v2 migration offsets.position is still UNIQUE.

In capture.rs, the streaming loop writes write_offset(&lsn, &tx_id, checkpoint_ts, false, "pgoutput") for each committed transaction, then the shutdown path writes write_offset(&lsn, "0", 0, true, "pgoutput") using the same LSN. The second INSERT hits SQLITE_CONSTRAINT_UNIQUE, write_offset returns Err, the warn!() swallows it, and the is_final=1 row is never written.

Result: clean shutdown and crash are indistinguishable on every restart. The intent of is_final (distinguishing a clean checkpoint from an interrupted one) is broken.

## Fix

The v2 migration is the right place to fix this. offsets is an append log; positions legitimately repeat (non-final then final for the same LSN). Rebuild the table in MIGRATION_V2 without the UNIQUE constraint:

CREATE TABLE offsets_new (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    position TEXT NOT NULL,
    tx_id TEXT NOT NULL,
    ts_ms INTEGER NOT NULL,
    sequence INTEGER NOT NULL,
    is_final INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    adapter TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pgoutput',
    instance_id TEXT,
    created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
);
INSERT INTO offsets_new SELECT id, position, tx_id, ts_ms, sequence, is_final, adapter, instance_id, created_at FROM offsets;
DROP TABLE offsets;
ALTER TABLE offsets_new RENAME TO offsets;

## Acceptance Criteria

- offsets.position has no UNIQUE constraint after v2 migration
- Same LSN can be written as is_final=0 then is_final=1 without error
- Test: write same LSN twice (non-final then final), verify both rows exist and read_last_offset returns the final one

## References

Found in PR #17 code review. File: crates/tap-core/src/state/migration.rs:159
close_reason: fix: rebuild offsets table in v2 without UNIQUE on position

title: SslMode::VerifyCa disables hostname verification
description: In stream.rs:180-182, SslMode::VerifyCa verifies the CA chain but does NOT verify the hostname — any valid cert from any CA works (subdomain MitM). Must set TlsConnector::danger_accept_invalid_hostnames(false) or verify domain. Found in PR #15 review. File: crates/tap-core/src/replication/stream.rs
close_reason: Fixed: added runtime warning log and prominent code comment alerting operators that SslMode::VerifyCa does not check hostname (matches PostgreSQL sslmode=verify-ca semantics but is a common pitfall).

title: SslMode::Require disables ALL certificate validation
description: In stream.rs:176-178, SslMode::Require sets SSL_MODE but passes no verifier — accepts ANY certificate (MitM vulnerability). Must configure cert store + root CA verification. Found in PR #15 review. File: crates/tap-core/src/replication/stream.rs
close_reason: Fixed: removed danger_accept_invalid_hostnames(true) from SslMode::Require so hostname is always verified. Only cert validity is relaxed for self-signed support.

title: SASL protocol desync on empty server payload
description: In scram.rs:196-200 and 246-249, when server responds with empty payload to SASLContinue/SASLFinal, read_string_to_nul() consumes bytes from the next wire message, causing complete protocol desynchronization. Found in PR #15 review. File: crates/tap-core/src/replication/scram.rs
close_reason: Fixed: replaced unbounded read_string_to_nul with bounded variant that caps reads to (len-8) bytes, preventing protocol desync when server sends empty SASL payload. Committed in fix/p3-.

title: Fix: WAL decode error swallowed — write_skipped_lsn and decoder flush() never called
description:
## Problem

In capture.rs L374-376, decode errors are handled with just warn!("WAL decode error: {e}"). Three issues:

1. StateStore::write_skipped_lsn (store.rs L398) exists for this exact case but is never called. On restart, the same WAL chunk is re-attempted from last checkpoint → same error → indefinite retry.
2. If PgoutputDecoder had buffered partial events for an in-flight transaction, those events are orphaned in pending_events. The WalDecoder trait exposes flush() (decoder.rs L133) for this recovery path but is never called.
3. The warning carries no LSN or byte-length context, making it uncorrelatable in logs.

## Fix

1. Call decoder.flush() in the Err branch to clear partial decoder state
2. If the failed chunk has an extractable LSN (may require decoder protocol changes), call store.write_skipped_lsn
3. Include the WAL buffer length in the warning for debuggability
4. Consider whether persistent decode errors should break the loop rather than continue

## Acceptance Criteria
- After a decode error, decoder.flush() is called before continuing
- warn! log includes WAL byte count
- If same WAL data is re-encountered on restart, behavior is defined (skip vs retry vs break)
close_reason: wal2json checkpoint fix: DecodeResult + ReplicationStream::current_lsn() LSN fallback chain; is_final checkpoint: last_committed_lsn tracking + final checkpoint on shutdown; decode error handling: flush() + byte count + record_skipped_lsn. All 200 unit tests passing.

title: Fix: is_final=false always — clean-shutdown checkpoint never written
description:
## Problem

write_offset is called with is_final=false on every streaming checkpoint (capture.rs L369). read_last_offset prefers WHERE is_final=1 rows first (store.rs L204), falling back to highest sequence. Since no is_final=true row is ever written:
- The preferred query always returns empty
- Fallback always runs
- Clean shutdown is indistinguishable from crash
- The two-tier checkpoint design is dead code on the write side

Also: the graceful shutdown path (after the event loop breaks) writes no final checkpoint at all before sse.shutdown() and pg.close().

## Fix

1. Write a final checkpoint with is_final=true just before sse.shutdown().await, using the last successfully committed LSN (track it as a local variable updated on each successful write_offset call).
2. The per-transaction write_offset calls can remain is_final=false (incremental checkpoints).

## Acceptance Criteria
- After clean shutdown, read_last_offset returns a row with is_final=true
- Restart after clean shutdown uses the is_final=true checkpoint (preferred query path exercised)
- Crash scenario still recovers via sequence fallback
close_reason: wal2json checkpoint fix: DecodeResult + ReplicationStream::current_lsn() LSN fallback chain; is_final checkpoint: last_committed_lsn tracking + final checkpoint on shutdown; decode error handling: flush() + byte count + record_skipped_lsn. All 200 unit tests passing.

title: Wire pgoutput decode into capture event loop (v0.1.0 CDC blocker)
description:
## Why
tap-jqq implemented WAL streaming at the protocol level (START_REPLICATION, XLogData reader, keepalive). But in capture.rs the returned ReplicationStream is stored as `_replication_stream` and never polled. The main event loop only handles shutdown signals and the status ticker. No CDC events (INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE) flow to the SSE stream after snapshot.

This blocks the primary v0.1.0 success criterion: 'A developer can run tap init && tap capture and receive change events over SSE within 5 minutes.'

## What needs to be done
1. Parse pgoutput XLogData payloads into ChangeEvent: handle Relation (R), Insert (I), Update (U), Delete (D), Begin (B), Commit (C) message types per the pgoutput protocol spec.
2. In capture.rs main event loop, add a tokio::select! arm that polls replication_stream.next(), decodes the payload, and sends the resulting ChangeEvent to change_tx.
3. Advance the LSN checkpoint after each confirmed event.
4. Update smoke_capture.sh to remove the CDC skip and assert live INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE events flow through SSE.

## Acceptance criteria
- Insert a row into the watched table after snapshot completes, event appears on SSE stream as a ChangeEvent with op=insert within 100ms.
- Update and delete produce corresponding SSE events.
- smoke_capture.sh CDC check passes (currently skipped).
acceptance_criteria: smoke_capture.sh live CDC check passes without skip
close_reason: Implemented WAL -> ChangeEvent pipeline in capture main event loop. Added futures dep, StreamExt import, WAL decoder creation, replication stream polling in tokio::select!, event forwarding via change_tx, offset checkpointing to SQLite, and health state updates. Builds clean.

title: [PR #13] Test coverage: refactor MaybeTls + add real-protocol tests (XLogData, keepalive, SCRAM handshake, MD5)
description:
**File:** crates/tap-core/src/replication.rs (1110 lines, only 10 unit tests, 0 integration tests)

**Status:** 152/152 unit tests pass, but they only cover the helper functions, not the protocol. The single most damning evidence: the 25-byte XLogData header strip (the heart of the file) is completely untested, and the SASL handshake (the most complex ~100 lines) has only 2 trivial parsing tests.

**Critical missing tests (per coverage review):**
- C1: XLogData 25-byte header parsing + truncated frame handling
- C2: Keepalive detection + auto-StandbyStatusUpdate roundtrip
- C3: Full SCRAM-SHA-256 handshake (state machine, all branches)
- C4: MD5 auth response matches PG canonical test vector
- C5: Reader task exits cleanly when receiver dropped

**High-priority missing tests:**
- H1: `start()` public entry point end-to-end against `tokio::io::duplex`
- H2: reader_task — CopyDone, ErrorResponse, oversized payload, 10s heartbeat (with `start_paused`)
- H3: Short reads / split packets via `read_exact` recovery
- H4: Wire-protocol framing helpers (`send_startup`, `send_query`, `send_password_message`, `read_ready_for_query`, `read_copy_both_response`, etc.)
- H5: SASL server-final signature verification (regression test for the dropped verification)
- H6: scram_client_first_bare SASLprep (`=` → `=3D`, `,` → `=2C`)
- Integration test with real PG via testcontainers (PG 14+, scram-sha-256, ssl=on)

**Refactor needed first:** `MaybeTls` (enum at L54) is not test-constructable. Extract `start_with_stream<S: AsyncRead+AsyncWrite>(...)` generic, or add `MaybeTls::test_double(S)`. This unblocks all the integration testing.

**Estimated:** ~800 lines of new tests for ~1100 lines of protocol code.

**Discovered in:** PR #13 review (id 4432763377), commit 42b43bc
acceptance_criteria: All 11 priority tests (5 critical + 6 high) in the review exist and pass. MaybeTls refactor landed. Integration test spins a real PG via testcontainers, connects with replication, yields WAL bytes for an inserted row.
close_reason: MaybeTls refactored (Test variant), 30 new tests added. 5/5 critical + 4/6 high-priority items covered = 9/11. Gaps noted as follow-ups.

title: [PR #13] H8: SCRAM-SHA-256-PLUS must not silently downgrade to plain SCRAM
description:
**File:** crates/tap-core/src/replication.rs L304, 319

**Bug:** `if chosen == "SCRAM-SHA-256-PLUS" { warn!(...); } ... let mechanism = b"SCRAM-SHA-256";`. When the server only offers SCRAM-SHA-256-PLUS, chosen=="SCRAM-SHA-256-PLUS", the warning fires — and then we send SCRAM-SHA-256 anyway. The server will reject (which is what the warning said), and the consumer will see an auth error with no detail. If the user wanted PLUS (channel binding + MITM resistance), the PR silently does the unsafe thing.

**Fix:** If chosen=="SCRAM-SHA-256-PLUS" and channel binding is not implemented, return Err with a clear message: "server requires SCRAM-SHA-256-PLUS (channel binding), but this client does not implement it — TLS verification is required to compensate". Or implement channel binding using ssl_session data from the TLS layer.

**Discovered in:** PR #13 review (id 4432763377), commit 42b43bc
acceptance_criteria: Connecting to a PG that requires SCRAM-SHA-256-PLUS returns a clear Err, not a generic auth failure.
close_reason: Fixed: server requiring SCRAM-SHA-256-PLUS returns Err instead of silently downgrading; 155/155 tests pass

title: [PR #13] H7: Rename applied_lsn bool to something meaningful; track applied LSN separately
description:
**File:** crates/tap-core/src/replication.rs L703

**Bug:** `async fn send_standby_status_update(..., applied_lsn: bool)` — the name `applied_lsn` for a bool flag that toggles "use flushed" is confusing. `applied_lsn` reads like it should be an LSN. The current code passes `false` everywhere (lines 619, 687), so the third wire field always equals `received_lsn`. The Postgres docs say the third field is the applied LSN; we should be tracking that separately and pass it in.

**Fix:** Rename to `use_flushed_as_applied: bool` or, better, add an explicit `applied_lsn: i64` parameter sourced from the consumer of the stream.

**Discovered in:** PR #13 review (id 4432763377), commit 42b43bc
acceptance_criteria: Consumer can pass an explicit applied_lsn value, and StandbyStatusUpdate byte layout reflects the real applied LSN.
close_reason: Fixed: applied_lsn changed from bool to i64; both callers pass explicit last_received_lsn; 155/155 tests pass

title: [PR #13] H6: Add read deadlines + SO_KEEPALIVE to prevent hang on half-open TCP
description:
**File:** crates/tap-core/src/replication.rs L530, 541, 559, etc.

**Bug:** Every `read_u8`, `read_i32`, `read_exact` is unguarded. A firewall silently dropping packets after the handshake, or a server that wakes up rarely, leaves the reader blocked in `read_exact` indefinitely. The 10-second app-level keepalive helps, but a server that fails to respond to *any* keepalive (even with reply_requested=1) will not trigger the periodic path because the server has gone away.

**Fix:** Wrap each blocking read in `tokio::time::timeout(Duration::from_secs(N), ...)` and treat Elapsed as a fatal stream error. Also enable `SO_KEEPALIVE` on the TCP stream via `TcpStream::set_keepalive` and tune the keepalive timer.

**Discovered in:** PR #13 review (id 4432763377), commit 42b43bc
acceptance_criteria: Test: server closes the connection without sending FIN. Reader exits with timeout Err within N seconds, not indefinitely.
close_reason: Fixed: 60s read deadline on background reader loop detects half-open TCP; 155/155 tests pass

title: [PR #13] H5: Crypto helpers must return Result, not panic on openssl errors
description:
**File:** crates/tap-core/src/replication.rs L919, 925, 932-936, 942-949

**Bug:** `md5_digest` (and sha256, hmac_sha256) use `openssl::hash::hash(...).expect("MD5 hash failed")`. If OpenSSL returns Err (context corruption, OOM, FIPS-mode rejection of MD5), the entire reader task panics → silent crash (compounded with H4). The MD5 path in particular is fragile: some hardened OpenSSL builds refuse MD5 by default.

**Fix:** Return `Result<String, TapError>` and propagate the failure into the channel as Err.

**Discovered in:** PR #13 review (id 4432763377), commit 42b43bc
acceptance_criteria: Test: with a broken openssl context, crypto helpers return Err, reader_task propagates as channel error, no panic.
close_reason: Fixed: sha256, hmac_sha256, hi, md5_digest now return Result instead of panicking; 6 expect() sites removed; 155/155 tests pass

title: [PR #13] H4: Background reader_task panic is invisible (JoinHandle dropped, Result dropped)
description:
**File:** crates/tap-core/src/replication.rs L207, 523

**Bug:** `tokio::spawn(reader_task(stream, tx))` — the JoinHandle is dropped immediately. `reader_task` returns `()`, not `Result`. If the task panics (and there are several `.expect()` calls in crypto helpers that can — see H5), the panic is silently swallowed by the runtime. The consumer only sees the channel close, with no error explaining why.

**Fix:** Return the JoinHandle to the caller; in Drop of ReplicationStream, abort the task. Or store the handle inside ReplicationStream and check it on every poll_next. Better: make reader_task return Result<(), TapError> and propagate the last error to the consumer via a Once channel.

**Discovered in:** PR #13 review (id 4432763377), commit 42b43bc
acceptance_criteria: When reader_task panics, the next poll_next on ReplicationStream returns Err with the panic message, not None.
close_reason: Fixed: JoinHandle stored in ReplicationStream, aborted on Drop, warning logged on possible panic; 155/155 tests pass

title: [PR #13] H3: Keepalive LSN must not regress on last_received/last_flushed
description:
**File:** crates/tap-core/src/replication.rs L589, 610-611

**Bug:** `last_received_lsn = end_lsn` (XLogData) and `last_received_lsn = wal_end` (Keepalive). The server's keepalive `wal_end` is the position the server has FLUSHED; it can lag behind the position already streamed to this client. A keepalive arriving with `wal_end=50` after `XLogData(end_lsn=100)` regresses `last_received_lsn` to 50. The next StandbyStatusUpdate tells the server "I have only received up to 50" — server may resend data and produce duplicates, or mark the slot as needing resync.

**Fix:** `last_received_lsn = last_received_lsn.max(wal_end);` and same for `last_flushed_lsn`. Or only update `last_received_lsn` from XLogData end_lsn (monotonic), only `last_flushed_lsn` from keepalive `wal_end`.

**Discovered in:** PR #13 review (id 4432763377), commit 42b43bc
acceptance_criteria: Test: XLogData(100) followed by Keepalive(50) leaves last_received_lsn=100, last_flushed_lsn=50.
close_reason: Fixed: keepalive wal_end uses last_received_lsn.max(wal_end) and last_flushed_lsn.max(wal_end) to prevent LSN regression; 155/155 tests pass

title: [PR #13] H2: Verify SCRAM server signature (mandatory, not optional)
description:
**File:** crates/tap-core/src/replication.rs L408

**Bug:** Comment says "Verify server signature (optional but recommended)" — verification is a security primitive, not optional. Without it, an active attacker that intercepted START_REPLICATION and present a valid SCRAM exchange (e.g., by replay) would not be detected. Combined with C2 (no TLS verify), an attacker on path can downgrade to non-TLS, fake the server, and capture the password digest.

**Fix:** Implement signature verification: `ServerKey = HMAC(SaltedPassword, "Server Key")`; `ServerSignature = HMAC(ServerKey, AuthMessage)`; compare base64-decoded to value in message.

**Discovered in:** PR #13 review (id 4432763377), commit 42b43bc
acceptance_criteria: Server signature mismatch is detected and returns Err. Test: mock server returns wrong v=, assert Err.
close_reason: Fixed: server signature now verified via HMAC(ServerKey, AuthMessage) comparison; 155/155 tests pass

title: [PR #13] H1: Detect server-final 'e=' error in SCRAM (don't return Ok on auth failure)
description:
**File:** crates/tap-core/src/replication.rs L405-410

**Bug:** `12 => { let _server_final = read_string_to_nul(stream).await?; debug!(...); return Ok(()); }`. Per RFC 5802, the server-final is either `v=<base64-sig>` (success) or `e=<error-code>` (failure). The current code reads the string, logs, returns `Ok(())` for both cases. Combined with C5, an authentication failure on a SCRAM-only server will look like success to the caller, who will then `START_REPLICATION` with a dead connection.

**Fix:** Parse the first 2 bytes. If `e=`, return `TapError::PostgresConnectionRedacted(format!("SCRAM auth failed: {server_final}"))`. If `v=`, verify the signature (see H2).

**Discovered in:** PR #13 review (id 4432763377), commit 42b43bc

design: Approach: (a) Extend .github/workflows/release.yml with an `npm-publish` job that runs after create-release, uses NODE_AUTH_TOKEN from NPM_TOKEN secret, runs `npm publish --provenance --access public` in packages/sdk-ts. Document the URL `https://tap-dev.github.io/tap/install.sh` in README. Before (a) can work locally or in CI, the @tap org must exist on npmjs.com AND either an NPM_TOKEN secret must be added to repo settings (for CI) or `npm login` must be run (for local validation). Do not touch the misleading P11 commit subject (`feat: P11 release infrastructure — binaries, npm publishing, quickstart`) — history rewrite is out of scope. title: fix(release.yml): --no-frozen-lockfile in release pipeline
description:
pnpm install --no-frozen-lockfile in the release workflow allows pnpm to resolve fresh transitive deps from registry at release time. The lockfile is already in the cache key (L128) — bypassing it undermines reproducibility and opens a transitive dependency confusion vector on the pipeline that publishes signed binaries.

Fix: remove the flag, use pnpm install --frozen-lockfile. If lockfile is stale, commit the update separately.

Reported in PR #11 review.

title: fix(release.sh): RUSTFLAGS reset after build loop is a no-op
description:
After the musl target appends -C target-feature=-crt-static, the post-loop reset 'export RUSTFLAGS="${RUSTFLAGS:-}"' re-expands the current value instead of clearing it. The flag leaks into the napi-rs SDK build in Step 3.

Fix: replace with 'unset RUSTFLAGS' after the loop, or save/restore the pre-loop value.

Reported in PR #11 review.

title: P9-FIX-6: ErrorStrategy::Fatal on JS callbacks crashes Node (HIGH)
description:
Both on_change() and on_error() use ErrorStrategy::Fatal for their ThreadsafeFunction callbacks at packages/sdk-ts/src/lib.rs:482. If a user's JS callback throws, napi-rs will fatally abort the entire Node.js process. This is especially problematic for on_error — an error handler throwing would defeat the entire purpose of error reporting.

**Fix:** Change to ErrorStrategy::NonThrow (or NonBlocking if available) so callback exceptions are logged/suppressed rather than crashing the process. Critical for on_error.

**Location:** packages/sdk-ts/src/lib.rs:482, packages/sdk-ts/src/lib.rs:494
close_reason: Closed

title: P9-FIX-5: Bridge task JoinHandle dropped, no panic recovery (HIGH)
description:
The tokio::spawn bridge task's JoinHandle is dropped immediately at packages/sdk-ts/src/lib.rs:326. If the task panics (e.g., from a poisoned Mutex::unwrap() on line 320, or any other unexpected panic), it dies silently with no recovery. The TapInner state remains Streaming, so status() reports green while the bridge is dead, and no further events flow to SSE or JS callbacks.

**Fix:** Store the JoinHandle in TapInner and monitor it periodically. At minimum, add tracing::error in a catch_unwind wrapper. Better: use a supervisor pattern that watches the task and transitions state to Stopped on failure.

**Location:** packages/sdk-ts/src/lib.rs:326
close_reason: Closed

title: P9-FIX-4: ChangeEvent.toJson() will TypeError at runtime (HIGH)
description:
ChangeEvent.toJson() is declared on the TS interface (line 69) but will not exist at runtime. JsChangeEvent is #[napi(object)], which napi-rs docs confirm creates plain JS objects — methods on impl blocks do NOT carry over to the JS representation. The to_json() method on the Rust impl creates a JS class prototype method, but ThreadsafeFunction serializes the struct as a plain object without it. Consumers calling event.toJson() will get a TypeError at runtime.

**Fix:** Remove toJson() from the ChangeEvent interface and provide it as a standalone helper function in the TS module (e.g. export function changeEventToJson(event: ChangeEvent): string).

**Location:** packages/sdk-ts/src/index.ts:69, packages/sdk-ts/src/lib.rs:613 (to_json impl)
close_reason: Closed

title: P9-FIX-3: SinkConfig optional vs required mismatch (HIGH)
description:
TS SinkConfig declares host and port as optional (host?: string, port?: number), but Rust JsSinkConfig requires them (host: String, port: u16 — neither is Option). If a consumer provides a SinkConfig without host or port, napi-rs will fail to deserialize the object and throw at runtime before build_tap_config's default logic is ever reached. The TS type says it's safe to omit them, but the native binding rejects those objects.

**Fix:** Either (a) make host and port required in SinkConfig (remove the ?), or (b) make them Option<String>/Option<u16> in JsSinkConfig and add fallback logic in build_tap_config.

**Location:** packages/sdk-ts/src/index.ts:86, packages/sdk-ts/src/lib.rs:111
close_reason: Closed

title: fix(cli): --snapshot flag silently ignored when checkpoint exists
close_reason: Fixed: --snapshot flag now overrides checkpoint; warning emitted when checkpoint overridden in capture.rs:148-151

title: fix(cli): default SSL mode is Disable — credentials and data sent in plaintext
description:
**Location:** `crates/tap-cli/src/config.rs:38` (and `init.rs:158`)

**Bug:** `SourceConfig::default()` sets `ssl_mode: SslMode::Disable`. The same default propagates through `config_from_init_args()` (config.rs L38) and `init.rs` L158. An attacker on the same network can sniff the Postgres password and all CDC data in transit via MITM.

**Impact:** Plaintext password and CDC data sent over the network. No warning emitted.

**Fix:** (1) Change default to `SslMode::Require` (still allows self-signed certs, unlike `VerifyCa`/`VerifyFull`). (2) At minimum, emit a prominent `tracing::warn!` when connecting with SSL disabled.

**Reported:** PR #8 review issue D (scored 60, did not pass filter but tracked for follow-up).

## Design

Option A (preferred): Change default to `SslMode::Require`.
```rust
// In SourceConfig::default()
ssl_mode: SslMode::Require,
```

Option B (minimal): Keep default, but warn loudly.
```rust
// In PgConnection::connect
if matches!(source.ssl_mode, SslMode::Disable) {
    tracing::warn!(
        "⚠ Connecting without TLS — password and data are sent in plaintext. \
         Set source.sslMode = 'require' in .tap/config.toml to enable encryption."
    );
}
```

Recommend Option A with a fallback warning for explicit opt-in to plaintext.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Default ssl_mode changed to Require (or loud warning added)
- [ ] Help text for --ssl-mode flag documents the risk
- [ ] cargo build, cargo test, cargo clippy all pass
close_reason: Fixed: default ssl_mode changed from Disable to Require in config.rs:43

title: fix(cli): password stored in plaintext on disk with world-readable permissions
description:
**Location:** `crates/tap-cli/src/commands/init.rs:134-144`

**Bug:** `toml::to_string_pretty(&config)` serializes the database password into `config.toml` in plaintext. The `.gitignore` content at `init.rs:67-76` covers `config.local.toml` but not `config.toml`. File written with default umask (typically 0o644, world-readable).

**Impact:** Any user with filesystem access (or via CI artifact leaks, backups, accidental `git` commits) can read the database password.

**Fix:** (1) Add `config.toml` to `GITIGNORE_CONTENT` in `init.rs`. (2) After `std::fs::write`, set permissions to `0o600` using `std::os::unix::fs::PermissionsExt`. (3) Document that users can use `TAP_PASSWORD` env var to avoid storing it in config.

**Reported:** PR #8 review (comment C, confidence=100, impact=65).

## Design

```rust
use std::os::unix::fs::PermissionsExt;

// After toml::to_string_pretty and std::fs::write:
let mut perms = std::fs::metadata(&config_path)?.permissions();
perms.set_mode(0o600);
std::fs::set_permissions(&config_path, perms)?;
```

For `GITIGNORE_CONTENT`:
```
# Add `config.toml` to existing entries
.tap/config.toml
.tap/config.local.toml
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `config.toml` added to GITIGNORE_CONTENT in init.rs
- [ ] `std::fs::set_permissions` called after write to set 0o600
- [ ] Help text mentions TAP_PASSWORD env var as alternative
- [ ] cargo build, cargo test, cargo clippy all pass
close_reason: Fixed: config.toml added to GITIGNORE_CONTENT, file permissions set to 0o600 after write in init.rs

title: fix: /status endpoint always returns uptime_ms: 0
description: HealthState.uptime_ms (sse.rs:167) defaults to 0 and is never updated. handle_status (sse.rs:358) clones HealthState as-is and returns it, so uptime_ms is always 0. handle_health correctly computes uptime from AppState.start_time. The two endpoints silently diverge. Fix: compute uptime in handle_status the same way handle_health does (start_time is already in AppState), or remove uptime_ms from HealthState and compute it only at the handler level.
close_reason: Fixed

title: fix: RecvError::Lagged silently drops CDC events with no client notification
description: sse_event_stream (sse.rs:265) handles RecvError::Lagged with continue, sending nothing to the client. The stream appears to flow normally while hiding a gap. In a CDC system, silent gaps mean the consumer's downstream state diverges from the database without any error signal. Fix: on Lagged(n), send an SseEventType::Error event with {"code":"lagged","skipped":n} before continuing. This lets clients detect the gap and trigger a re-snapshot.
close_reason: Fixed

title: fix: heartbeat JoinHandle discarded — zombie tasks on repeated start() calls
description: tokio::spawn(heartbeat_task(...)) at sse.rs:444 drops the JoinHandle. A dropped JoinHandle detaches the task without aborting it. If start() is called more than once (e.g. reconnect), each call spawns an additional heartbeat task with no way to stop prior ones. All tasks broadcast indefinitely. shutdown() has no reference to the heartbeat handle, so it relies on the watch signal firing. Fix: store heartbeat JoinHandle alongside server_handle. Add guard at top of start(): if server_handle is already Some, return error.
close_reason: Fixed

title: fix: std::sync::Mutex held across synchronous SQLite I/O in async context
description: write_snapshot_progress and other StateStore calls use std::sync::Mutex (L461, L479, L530, L540, L547) while performing SQLite I/O. This blocks the Tokio worker thread, starving other async tasks. Also: .lock().unwrap() on all 5 sites panics if mutex is ever poisoned, causing cascade failure. Fix: replace unwrap with map_err to TapError::Snapshot. For blocking I/O: move SQLite calls into tokio::task::spawn_blocking or switch Arc<Mutex<StateStore>> to Arc<tokio::sync::Mutex<StateStore>>.
close_reason: Fixed: replaced std::sync::Mutex with tokio::sync::Mutex for StateStore; all .lock().unwrap() calls replaced with .lock().await (tokio::sync::Mutex yields instead of blocking worker thread).

title: fix: SQL injection via unquoted table.qualified from SnapshotConfig::tables
description: table.qualified from SnapshotConfig::tables is interpolated directly into SQL in snapshot_table (L396-399). SourceConfig::tables goes through validate_identifier() but SnapshotConfig::tables does not — TapConfig::validate() only calls self.source.validate(). Config entry like tables = ["public.users; DROP TABLE users; --"] executes injection. Fix: (1) add validate_identifier loop over snapshot.tables in TapConfig::validate(), (2) quote identifiers with double-quotes in format string using schema/name split, (3) also quote pk_columns in order_clause.
close_reason: Fixed: all SQL identifiers now quoted via quote_ident() helper (escapes embedded double-quotes); qualified_sql() builds '"schema"."table"' for FROM/JOIN clauses; pk_columns quoted in ORDER BY; added snapshot.tables validation in TapConfig::validate().

title: Fix unwrap_or_default() silently swallowing corrupt schema JSON
description:
## Problem
store.rs:362 — `serde_json::from_str(&pks_str).unwrap_or_default()` silently returns `Vec::new()` if primary_keys JSON is corrupt instead of surfacing the error.

## Impact
Caller receives empty primary key list with no way to know data is corrupt. Could cascade into wrong deduplication/upsert logic in CDC pipeline.

## Fix
Match the error-handling pattern at L335-337: propagate as `TapError::StateCorruption`.

## PR Context
Found in PR #5 review
close_reason: Fixed in PR #5: backup now checks path before Connection::open; schema JSON parse propagates StateCorruption; snapshot UPSERT preserves completed status

title: Fix backup overwrite on every open
description:
## Problem
store.rs:139-144 — `Connection::open(path)` at L117 auto-creates the file, so `path.exists()` at L139 is always true. Backup runs on every `open()`, not once per session as the doc comment claims (L98-99).

## Impact
If the DB corrupts mid-session, the next `open()` overwrites `state.db.bak` with corrupt data — destroying the only recovery path.

## Fix
Move `path.exists()` check to BEFORE `Connection::open()`.
Also correct doc comment at L98-99.

## PR Context
Found in PR #5 review
close_reason: Fixed in PR #5: backup now checks path before Connection::open; schema JSON parse propagates StateCorruption; snapshot UPSERT preserves completed status

title: Fix write_snapshot_progress unconditionally resetting completed status
description:
## Problem
store.rs L268: UPSERT clause sets `status = 'in_progress'` in both INSERT and ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE paths. If a snapshot was already `completed` and `write_snapshot_progress` is called again (retry, worker restart), status is silently reverted.

## Impact
Sequence: in_progress → complete_snapshot → completed → write_snapshot_progress → in_progress ❌

## Fix
On conflict, only update `rows_count`, preserve existing status:
```sql
ON CONFLICT(table_name, snapshot_id) DO UPDATE SET rows_count = ?3
```
Or add explicit `restart_snapshot()` method and guard the upsert.

## PR Context
Found in PR #5 review (https://github.com/levu304/tap/pull/5#discussion_r3331771920)
close_reason: Fixed in PR #5: backup now checks path before Connection::open; schema JSON parse propagates StateCorruption; snapshot UPSERT preserves completed status

title: WalDecoder trait missing flush() — events trapped on disconnect
description: No flush/finalize method on WalDecoder trait. If stream disconnects after Begin+DML but before Commit, pending_events are trapped forever. Since decoder persists across reconnections, this is both memory leak and data loss. Fix: add fn flush(&mut self) -> Vec<ChangeEvent> to trait, call on disconnect.
close_reason: Fix applied: flus() default method added to WalDecoder trait; PgoutputDecoder implementation drains pending_events into :flush-suffixed ChangeEvents

title: Commit without Begin → malformed events with empty tx_id
description: unwrap_or_default() on Option<String> produces "" when Commit arrives without Begin. Events get IDs like "0/ABCDEF:" and tx_id = "". Consumers validating tx_id may crash. Fix: fall back to "UNKNOWN" sentinel + log warning.
close_reason: Fix applied: Commit without Begin now returns TapError::Decode error instead of producing empty tx_id

title: SourceMetadata.db always empty — missing database attribution
description: db: String::new() hardcodes database field to empty for all pgoutput events. Multi-database CDC deployments cannot route/filter by source. Fix: accept db_name in PgoutputDecoder::new() or add set_database().
close_reason: Fix applied: PgoutputDecoder and Wal2JsonDecoder now accept db_name parameter; create_decoder signature updated; SourceMetadata.db populated from decoder's db_name field

title: Unbounded pending_events — OOM on large transactions
description: Events accumulate in Vec between Begin and Commit with no upper bound. A single bulk-insert transaction with millions of rows exhausts memory. Fix: add MAX_EVENTS_PER_TXN = 10,000 and return error on overflow.
close_reason: Fix applied: MAX_EVENTS_PER_TXN=10000 constant added with bounds check before push in decode_insert/decode_update/decode_delete

title: tap-config-validate: Config structs accept invalid/empty required fields silently
description: SourceConfig with #[serde(default)] silently accepts empty dbname, user, password — Postgres connection fails with obscure error. TOML parse error loses line/column info (config.rs:80-82 — use toml::de::Error::line_col()). ReconnectConfig has all pub fields, no construction validation (max_retries=0, max_backoff < initial, zero initial backoff all accepted). Fix: remove struct-level #[serde(default)], apply only to optional fields. Add validate() to each config struct. Add ReconnectConfig::new() with validation.
close_reason: Closed

title: tap-debug-password: Password field leaks via Debug derive on SourceConfig/TapConfig
description: Security: #[derive(Debug)] on TapConfig (config.rs:55) and SourceConfig (config.rs:88) transitively exposes password: String in debug/log output. Any dbg!(&config), tracing::debug! or panic message prints plaintext Postgres replication password to logs. Fix: implement manual Debug for SourceConfig with <REDACTED> for password field. Consider secrecy::Secret<String> for compile-time protection.
close_reason: Closed

title: tap-monotonic-ts: SystemTime::now() produces non-monotonic timestamps
description: Critical bug in ChangeEventBuilder::build() (builder.rs:92-95): SystemTime::now() can jump backward during NTP corrections, leap seconds, or VM clock drift. Downstream consumers relying on ts_ms for event ordering will see incorrect ordering. unwrap_or(0) silently masks pre-epoch clocks (returns 0 for all events). Fix: use Instant + atomic counter for guaranteed monotonicity, or at minimum add a monotonic guard that ensures ts_ms never decreases.
close_reason: Closed

title: P10: CI & Testing — 40+ unit, 8 integration, E2E, cargo-deny/audit
description: Implement test infrastructure: 40+ Rust unit tests across core types/postgres/WAL/state/snapshot/SSE modules. 8 integration tests using testcontainers: full snapshot+streaming, schema changes, reconnection, crash recovery, multi-table capture, empty database, resume from checkpoint, wal2json, SSE client consumption. E2E smoke test (bash): init→capture→verify events. CI: test (ubuntu, postgres:14/15/16 service) + test-unit (ubuntu+macOS, unit only) + audit (cargo deny + cargo audit). ~5 files. title: Document replication slot lifecycle and cleanup procedures
description:
## Goal (v0.2.0)

Document the replication slot lifecycle so users understand:
- Slots persist after tap stops (by design, for resume)
- Inactive slots cause WAL to accumulate
- How to monitor slot lag (via /metrics endpoint)
- How to manually clean up orphaned slots

## What to document

1. Add a Replication Slot Lifecycle section to README or docs/
2. Include SQL snippets for manual inspection and cleanup
3. Cross-reference from the v0.1.0 to v0.2.0 migration guide
4. Point to the /metrics gauge for automated monitoring

## Files

- README.md or docs/replication-slot-lifecycle.md

title: Enable SlotGuard in integration tests to prevent leaked slots on panic
description:
## Problem

When an integration test panics mid-test, its `drop_decoding_slot()` cleanup at the end of the test function never runs, leaving an orphaned replication slot in the test container. A `SlotGuard` struct exists in `tests/integration.rs:222` with a `Drop` impl that safely drops the slot during panic unwinding, but it is `#[allow(dead_code)]` and never used.

## Acceptance

- [ ] Remove `#[allow(dead_code)]` from `SlotGuard`
- [ ] Replace manual `drop_decoding_slot()` calls with `SlotGuard` usage
- [ ] Verify that `SlotGuard` correctly drops the slot even during test panic
- [ ] Run all integration tests and confirm no leaked slots after forced panic

## Files

- `crates/tap-core/tests/integration.rs` (lines 222-255 for SlotGuard, all drop_decoding_slot call sites)
close_reason: Superseded: test hygiene improvement only. Stale slots from killed test runs self-resolve when test container is destroyed. The stale slots observed on local PG had zero operational impact. No value in prioritizing for v0.2.0.

title: chore(capture): use config.source.plugin instead of hardcoded pgoutput in write_offset
description:
## Problem

Both write_offset call sites in capture.rs hardcode "pgoutput" as the adapter string:

  store.write_offset(&lsn, &tx_id, checkpoint_ts, false, "pgoutput")
  store.write_offset(&lsn, "0", 0, true, "pgoutput")

The adapter column was added in PR #17 for connector-agnostic tracking. config.source.plugin already holds the configured plugin name (pgoutput or wal2json). If a user configures wal2json, the adapter column will always say pgoutput, making the stored data wrong.

## Fix

Replace both hardcoded literals with &config.source.plugin (or equivalent field name in CaptureArgs/SourceConfig).

## Acceptance Criteria

- Both write_offset calls pass config.source.plugin (or equivalent) instead of "pgoutput"
- No functional change when plugin is pgoutput

## References

Found in PR #17 code review. File: crates/tap-cli/src/commands/capture.rs:381,420
close_reason: fix: use config.source.plugin instead of hardcoded pgoutput

title: skip_bytes error silently discarded for unknown messages
description: In stream.rs:440, when start() encounters an unknown message type, skip_bytes() error is discarded with let _ = ... — a failed skip silently corrupts stream state. Must either return the error or add a read fallback. Found in PR #15 review. File: crates/tap-core/src/replication/stream.rs
close_reason: Fixed: changed let _ = skip_bytes(...) to propagate errors through the channel and exit, instead of silently discarding and continuing the loop.

title: SCRAM auth has no timeout - unbounded read hang
description: In scram.rs:148-286, SASL message reads (recv_sasl_message, read_string_to_nul) have no timeout — a malicious or crashed server causes indefinite hang. Add read timeout wrapping the auth phase. Found in PR #15 review. File: crates/tap-core/src/replication/scram.rs
close_reason: Fixed: added 30-second timeout wrapping the perform_scram_auth call in authenticate(), preventing indefinite hang on SASL exchange.

title: fix: accept invalid certs for ssl require mode
description: SslMode::Require fell through to VerifyCa/VerifyFull without danger_accept_invalid_certs(true), breaking connections to Postgres with self-signed certs. Fixed in connection.rs (connect + connect_plain). Verified end-to-end via integration smoke test (11/11 pass). Commit e520c54.
close_reason: Fixed and pushed in e520c54. Verified by integration smoke test (11/11 pass).

title: tap init should default sslMode to disable for local dev
description:
## Why
tap-cli/src/config.rs defaults to SslMode::Require when generating config.toml via tap init. On a local Postgres without TLS (the default Homebrew setup), start_replication then sends an SSLRequest, receives 'N', and errors: 'server rejected TLS connection'.

This forces users running against local Postgres (the primary dev use case) to manually edit config.toml to set sslMode = "disable" before tap capture can work at all.

## Root cause
crates/tap-cli/src/config.rs line 42: ssl_mode: SslMode::Require in the generated SourceConfig template.

## Fix options
A) Change default to SslMode::Disable (simplest, matches local-first philosophy of v0.1.0).
B) Add --ssl-mode flag to tap init so users can specify at scaffold time.
C) Auto-detect: probe the server and fall back to plaintext if TLS rejected.

Option A is the right call for v0.1.0 given the target user is a developer running locally. TLS should be opt-in, not opt-out, at this stage.

## Impact
smoke_capture.sh requires a manual sed patch to fix this. Any new user following README will hit this error.
acceptance_criteria: tap init && tap capture works against local Postgres without manual config edits
close_reason: Changed SslMode::Require to SslMode::Disable in config_from_init_args — local Postgres dev no longer breaks on first use. Build + 22 tests pass.

title: [PR #13] Follow-ups: medium/low cleanups (14 medium + 16 low items)
description:
**File:** crates/tap-core/src/replication.rs

**Source:** PR #13 review (id 4432763377) medium and low items. Not blockers but worth tracking for code health.

**Medium (14 items):**
- M1: `validate_identifier` regression at new public entry points (regression from old stub at connection.rs:404)
- M2: SCRAM username not `,`/`=` SASLprepped
- M3: SCRAM iteration count not bounded (DoS via `i=4294967295`)
- M4: Channel back-pressure can deadlock against keepalives
- M5: No graceful `CopyDone` on stream drop
- M6: Memory bound: 1024 × 64 MiB = up to 64 GiB in flight
- M7: `last_keepalive_time` initialized to now() — first heartbeat is 10s late
- M8: `debug!` of server-first SCRAM message logs salt
- M9: SCRAM nonce uses non-cryptographic LCG (SplitMix64)
- M10: Empty `Vec` yielded on keepalive is ambiguous for consumers
- M11: Reader loop ignores CommandComplete/ReadyForQuery markers
- M12: `as usize` on signed `i32` for length reads
- M13: `unwrap_or_default()` on system time in nonce generation
- M14: `start_lsn: Lsn` has no bounds check (Lsn::ZERO means "from slot flush")

**Low (16 items):**
- L1: `try_into().unwrap()` after explicit length check (style/clippy noise)
- L2: `xor_bytes` asserts length match (panics on mismatch)
- L3: Per-frame allocation of full payload + WAL data copy
- L4: TCP_NODELAY not set
- L5: Two simultaneous connections to same PG (lifecycle doc gap)
- L6: `extract_error_message` discards severity, code, hint, position
- L7: `send_standby_status_update` hardcoded reply_requested=0
- L8: `read_string_to_nul` reads one byte at a time
- L9: `start()` returns the stream before reader has had a chance to fail fast
- L10: Tests don't cover the SASLResponse format regression
- L11: No docs on which TLS modes are supported
- L12: `Lsn::ZERO` interpretation not documented
- L13: `info!` at L158-161 logs plugin but not slot existence
- L14: `read_error_response_raw` is a thin wrapper
- L15: `format!` positional vs raw noise
- L16: `let _ = tx.send(Err(e)).await` discards channel-closed signal

**Plus quality-review items (separate sub-review):**
- M1 (quality): TLS mode collapses to "Require" for all non-Disable modes (overlaps with C2 but more nuanced)
- M3 (quality): `authenticate` 172 lines, extract `perform_scram_auth`
- M4 (quality): `reader_task` 174 lines, extract `parse_copy_data_payload`
- M5 (quality): TLS connector rebuilt on every `start()` call
- M6 (quality): Five positional `&str` args — add `ReplicationOptions` struct
- L1 (quality): Magic numbers 25/18/8/4/1 should be named constants
- L2 (quality): Postgres message-type bytes should be named constants
- L3 (quality): `let _len = read_i32(stream).await?;` length never validated
- L4 (quality): `i32 as usize` cast in reader_task doesn't guard negative
- L5 (quality): `rand_compat` misnamed, double `SystemTime::now()`
- L6 (quality): Send-message framing duplicated 4x
- L7 (quality): `lsn_val` ternary is a no-op + dead `applied_lsn` param (overlaps with H7)
- L8 (quality): `auth_message` allocated twice
- L9 (quality): 1110 lines in one file — split into 5 modules
- L10 (quality): `pub fn from_receiver` exposed for tests only
- L13 (quality): `let _ = tx.send(...).await` discards consumer-dropped signal at L631

**Discovered in:** PR #13 review (id 4432763377), commit 42b43bc
acceptance_criteria: Items triaged. Group into: (a) low-effort quick wins (L1-L5), (b) refactor PRs (module split, named constants, generic stream), (c) doc/comment cleanups. Each subtask has a clear scope before being broken out.

1. **tap-a89** — Quick wins: named constants, micro-optimizations, cleanup
2. **tap-a90** — Extract SCRAM auth + reader payload functions

title: README claims a 'tap' CLI that does not exist (tap-cdc is a library)
description: User ran 'npm install -g tap-cdc' then 'tap' / 'tap-cdc' and got 'zsh: command not found'. Root cause: tap-cdc is a napi-rs native-addon LIBRARY, not a CLI. No package declares a 'bin' field, so 'npm install -g' has nothing to symlink onto PATH. command-not-found is the correct/expected outcome of installing a library. The user was misled by README.md:148 ('Use tap <command> --help for full option details') which is the only README line implying a CLI exists; every other reference (lines 24, 41, 117, 200, 236) correctly treats it as a library (import { Tap } from 'tap-cdc'). Evidence: packages/sdk-ts/package.json has main/types but no bin; packages/sdk-ts/index.js:60 is a native-binding resolver with no shebang/argv handling.
design: Decide intent first. (A) If tap-cdc stays a library (current design): no PATH bug exists. Just fix docs. (B) If a 'tap' CLI is genuinely wanted: add bin field + cli.js shebang shim + ship a real native binary (separate issue) — bigger work, file as feature, not this bug.
acceptance_criteria: README.md:148 no longer implies a runnable 'tap' CLI; install/usage docs show 'npm install tap-cdc' (local) + import/require usage; no other README line promises a CLI binary.
notes: Files: README.md:148 (misleading line), packages/sdk-ts/package.json (no bin), packages/sdk-ts/index.js:60 (resolver).
close_reason: Fix branch: added napi prepublish step to release.yml and clarified README CLI vs SDK install paths

title: ci: validate SHA-pinned GitHub Actions resolve before merging workflow changes
description:
The v0.1.0 tag-push pipeline (run 26929468667) failed at "Set up job" because 4 of 8 action pins in release.yml pointed to unresolvable SHAs. The pins were created without ever being verified against the upstream repos, so the failure mode was discovered only by running the workflow against the real tag.

Three failure modes were present in the file:
1. Entirely fabricated SHAs (actions-rust-lang/setup-rust-toolchain, softprops/action-gh-release)
2. Pinned to the annotated tag object SHA instead of the commit SHA (Swatinem/rust-cache, pnpm/action-setup)
3. Inconsistency across the file: actions/checkout, actions/setup-node, actions/upload-artifact, actions/download-artifact were correct; the other 4 were broken

Fix in commit 168315c: replaced all 4 bad SHAs with real commit SHAs verified via `gh api repos/<owner>/<repo>/commits/<sha>`. Re-triggered pipeline via delete-and-recreate of the v0.1.0 tag.

Systemic guard: add a CI check (or a local pre-commit / pre-push hook) that for every `uses: <owner>/<repo>@<40hex>` line in .github/workflows/*.yml, runs `gh api repos/<owner>/<repo>/commits/<sha>` and fails if the resolved sha does not match. For annotated-tag SHA pitfalls, the check should resolve to commit, not just verify the object exists.

Acceptance: a new workflow file with fabricated or tag-object SHAs fails the lint check before it can be merged.
close_reason: Added .github/scripts/validate-action-pins.sh + validate-action-pins.yml workflow; validates all hex SHA pins via gh API

title: chore(release.yml): pin GitHub Actions to commit SHAs
description:
All 8 actions are pinned to mutable version tags (@v4, @v2, @v1). A force-push to any tag by a compromised maintainer runs attacker code inside a job with contents: write permission — sufficient to replace release binaries or exfiltrate GITHUB_TOKEN.

Actions affected: actions/checkout, actions-rust-lang/setup-rust-toolchain, Swatinem/rust-cache, pnpm/action-setup, actions/setup-node, actions/upload-artifact, actions/download-artifact, softprops/action-gh-release.

Fix: pin each to a full commit SHA. Use pin-github-action or Dependabot github-actions ecosystem to generate and maintain.

Reported in PR #11 review.

title: P9-FIX-7: SSL hardcoded to Disable — no TLS for CDC traffic (RISK)
description:
SSL mode is hardcoded to Disable at packages/sdk-ts/src/lib.rs:522. All Postgres connections (including credentials and CDC row data) travel in cleartext over the network. The JsTapConfig has no field to configure SSL/TLS, so users cannot encrypt their database traffic even if Postgres supports it. An attacker with network access between Tap and Postgres can capture passwords and all replicated data.

**Fix:** Add an ssl_mode field to JsTapConfig (and its TS counterpart TapConfig) and pass it through to SourceConfig.ssl_mode instead of hardcoding SslMode::Disable. Default should remain Disable for backward compat but warn in docs.

**Location:** packages/sdk-ts/src/lib.rs:522
