title: tap-a94: Deferred — items needing discussion
description:
## Items
File: crates/tap-core/src/replication.rs

### Channel back-pressure deadlock against keepalives (M4)
- CHANNEL_CAPACITY=1024, MAX_MESSAGE_SIZE=64MiB → up to 64 GiB in flight
- Keepalive responses write to the same stream while the channel is full
- If consumer is slow, channel fills up, reader can't send keepalive response → server may disconnect
- Fix options: (a) separate keepalive channel, (b) bounded memory via semaphore, (c) drop old items

### Memory bound: 1024 × 64 MiB = 64 GiB (M6)
- No back-pressure mechanism. A fast producer can enqueue 64 GiB before any blocking
- Fix: link M4 solution to per-item size tracking

### No graceful CopyDone on stream drop (M5)
- ReplicationStream::drop aborts the reader task but doesn't send CopyDone to server
- Server wastes resources until keepalive timeout detects the disconnect
- Fix: send CopyDone before abort in Drop impl (needs AsyncWrite, tricky in sync Drop)

### debug! logs salt (M8) — security concern
- L481:  includes the salt in clear text
- While SCRAM salt isn't secret per se, logging it in production is unnecessary
- Fix: redact salt or log at trace level

### Non-cryptographic nonce / SplitMix64 (M9)
- generate_nonce uses SplitMix64, not a CSPRNG
- For SCRAM per RFC 5802 the nonce must be "fresh" and "unpredictable"
- SplitMix64 is predictable if attacker knows the seed (PID + nanosecond timestamp)
- Fix: use OsRng or thread_rng from rand crate for the nonce

### SCRAM iteration bound — DoS vector (M3)
- L1067: iteration count parsed as u32 with no upper bound
- Server could send i=4294967295 and client would compute PBKDF2 for hours
- Fix: cap iterations at reasonable maximum (e.g., 100000 or 1000000)

### Reader ignores CommandComplete/ReadyForQuery (M11)
- After CopyDone ('c'), server sends CommandComplete + ReadyForQuery before closing
- Reader currently exits on CopyDone without consuming trailing messages
- Socket may have unread bytes that accumulate

### TCP_NODELAY not set (L4)
- TcpStream created without disabling Nagle's algorithm
- Can cause latency on small writes (auth messages, keepalive responses)
- Fix:  after connect

### Two simultaneous connections (L5)
- No guard against opening two replication streams to the same slot
- Postgres allows multiple connections; consumer should be aware of the lifecycle implications

### let _ = tx.send(Err(e)).await discards signal (L16)
- When the receiver is dropped, tx.send() returns Err(...) which is discarded
- The reader keeps running, burning a task
- Fix: propagate the signal (reader exits on dropped rx)

### SCRAM username SASLprep (M2)
- L1042: username sent verbatim; RFC 5802 and RFC 4013 require SASLprep normalization
- Some PG deployments enforce this and will reject non-prepped usernames
- Fix: run config.user through SASLprep (stringprep) before SCRAM

### start_lsn: Lsn has no bounds check (M14)
- Lsn::ZERO means "from slot flush position"
- No validation that the provided LSN is reasonable (e.g., modulo alignment, max value)
- Fix: add validation in start() before sending to server
close_reason: Fixed: RFC 5802 §7 SASLprep escaping (',' → '=2C', '=' → '=3D') in scram_client_first_bare; 175/175 pass, clippy clean

title: refactor(cli): centralize .tap/config.toml default path constant
description:
**Locations (4 sites):**
- `crates/tap-cli/src/main.rs:63` (`Cli`)
- `crates/tap-cli/src/commands/capture.rs:27` (`CaptureArgs`)
- `crates/tap-cli/src/commands/inspect.rs:16` (`InspectArgs`)
- `crates/tap-cli/src/commands/test.rs:17` (`TestArgs`)

The string `".tap/config.toml"` appears as the `default_value` in four separate `#[arg()]` attributes. Changing the default path requires updating all four sites.

**Impact:** DRY violation. Maintenance burden if the path ever changes.

**Fix:** Define a public constant in `config.rs` and reference it from all four `#[arg(default_value = ...)]` attributes.

**Reported:** PR #8 review code-quality finding #3 (did not pass PR filter but actionable cleanup).

## Design

```rust
// In config.rs
pub const DEFAULT_CONFIG_PATH: &str = ".tap/config.toml";

// In all four sites
#[arg(long, default_value = crate::config::DEFAULT_CONFIG_PATH)]
config: PathBuf,
```

Note: clap's `default_value` accepts a `&str` constant, so a `pub const` works directly.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `DEFAULT_CONFIG_PATH` constant defined in `config.rs`
- [ ] All four `#[arg(default_value = ...)]` attributes reference the constant
- [ ] No behavioral change (default path is the same)
- [ ] cargo build, cargo test, cargo clippy all pass
close_reason: Fixed: DEFAULT_CONFIG_PATH constant in config.rs, referenced from all 5 CLI arg structs via default_value_t

title: chore(cli): clean up stale #[allow] attributes and dead code

2. Remove `ordinal_position` field from `ColumnInfo` (L35-36) — the `ORDER BY ordinal_position` in SQL still works

4. Replace misleading comment at `inspect.rs:313-314` with `// Used in run()`

**Reported:** PR #8 review code-quality findings (scored by code-reviewer, did not pass PR filter but actionable cleanup).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Stale `#[allow(...)]` attributes removed
- [ ] Dead `ordinal_position` field removed (or exposed in TS types if useful)
- [ ] Misleading comment fixed
- [ ] cargo build, cargo test, cargo clippy all pass

title: refactor(cli): validate --from-lsn at CLI boundary
description:
**Location:** `crates/tap-cli/src/commands/capture.rs:57-59` and `capture.rs:79-81`

**Bug:** The CLI `--from-lsn` value is an unvalidated `String` that's stored directly into `config.capture.from_lsn`. The LSN parse error only surfaces at L81, AFTER config load, PG connection, and state store open have all completed. Wastes user time on every typo or invalid format.

**Impact:** Delayed error feedback. No data corruption. Just bad UX.

**Fix:** Parse LSN at the CLI boundary (clap value_parser or post-parse validation). Store `Option<Lsn>` (the parsed value) in config. Validate early with a clear error message.

**Reported:** PR #8 review issue H (scored 25, did not pass filter but actionable cleanup).

## Design

```rust
// In CaptureArgs
#[arg(long, value_parser = parse_lsn)]
from_lsn: Option<Lsn>,

fn parse_lsn(s: &str) -> Result<Lsn, String> {
    s.parse::<Lsn>().map_err(|e| format!("invalid --from-lsn '{}': {}", s, e))
}
```

Then `config.capture.from_lsn` becomes `Option<Lsn>` (already parsed). Remove the parse step at L81.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `--from-lsn` validated at CLI boundary with clear error message
- [ ] Config stores parsed `Option<Lsn>` instead of unvalidated String
- [ ] Removed LSN parse from runtime path (L81)
- [ ] Test added for invalid LSN format
- [ ] cargo build, cargo test, cargo clippy all pass
close_reason: Fixed: validate_lsn_string() value_parser validates LSN format at CLI parse time in capture.rs:30-33

title: chore: fix false HTTP/2 claim in SSE server docs — server is HTTP/1.1
description: Module doc (sse.rs:3) and SseServer struct doc (sse.rs:367) describe the server as HTTP/2. axum::serve over a plain TcpListener negotiates HTTP/1.1. HTTP/2 requires TLS with ALPN or explicit h2c upgrade, neither of which is configured. The integration tests confirm this (they send HTTP/1.1 requests). Fix: change all references from HTTP/2 to HTTP/1.1 in module doc, struct doc, and PR description. If HTTP/2 is genuinely needed, configure TLS via axum-server + rustls.
close_reason: Fixed

value:
SnapshotRunner deadlock: drop runner before awaiting connection handles in tokio-postgres

connect_plain() returns Client + JoinHandle. The JoinHandle only resolves after the connection driver task completes, which only happens after Client is dropped. If Client is moved into SnapshotRunner (or any struct) and the handles are awaited while the struct is still alive, it creates a tokio self-deadlock. Fix: drop(runner) before handle.await. Affected: integration tests (test_snapshot_runner_captures_existing_rows) and capture.rs.
